blocks.content:
# Forecast Notes - 2026-01-02

State/market:
- `state.json` absent. `market.csv`: 330 rows, 323 active, 7 resolved, no active rows without a prediction.
- Newly resolved since Jan 1 notes: Bangladesh T20 relocation country `Sri Lanka`; Venezuela interim president `Delcy Rodriguez`.

Submitted updates:
- `709715`: Added `Kyrylo Budanov` as top outcome. Al Jazeera/Straits/HT reported Zelenskyy named GUR chief Budanov as chief of staff/top aide on Jan 2. This may be the resolving appointment even though the market title says economic development adviser.
- `797906`: Shifted Yemen integration body back toward `National Shield Forces` variants after Al Jazeera said Hadramout governor was put over National Shield forces; Reuters/Straits still used Homeland Shield wording, so kept both variants.
- `69214`: Raised `Lorestan` further and lifted `Chaharmahal and Bakhtiari`; Jan 2 Iran protest reports named deaths in Azna/Kouhdasht (Lorestan) and Lordegan (Chaharmahal and Bakhtiari), with protests across 17 provinces.
- `663866`: Raised `Italy` and `France` for Crans-Montana protest country. Italian FM visited; first named victim was Italian; reports said Italian and French nationals were missing/injured and owners were French nationals.
- `45659`: Moved Grok-ban question to `India`/`France`. India issued a 72-hour official notice to X/Grok over unlawful explicit content; French ministers referred Grok content to prosecutors and Arcom.
- `655453`, `48762`, `575662`: Nudged Russia-Ukraine-US talks mediation/location toward Turkey/Istanbul/Ankara after Umerov met Hakan Fidan in Ankara to discuss the negotiation process and next steps.
- `474377`: Put `Ruben Amorim` ahead of Michael Carrick for who takes Man United in the Jan 17 derby; Jan 2 Sky/Independent pieces still had Amorim actively managing and downplaying January change.
- `599463`, `915183`: Nudged toward `Tim Walz`/`Minnesota` after multiple Jan 2 federal-scrutiny articles on Minnesota fraud, voter records, and immigration-linked reviews.

Skipped:
- ICE target state `214384`: Jan 2 reporting focused on Phoenix/Arizona, but the active question asks for a northeastern state.
- Gaza/Board of Peace/Palestinian committee questions: Rafah/Jolie/aid-restriction articles did not identify the requested body, leader, member country, or evacuation destination.
- Tennis draw questions: Venus wildcard and Boulter coaching articles did not yet identify Australian Open draws or opponents.
- Marc Guehi: still interest/rumour only, no agreement in principle.

The Africa Cup of Nations is Africa’s main men’s international football tournament. As the knockout rounds unfold, the bracket will determine which team meets the host nation in the semifinal. criteria:
  • Source of Truth: CAF’s official AFCON 2025 bracket and match schedule on CAFOnline.com. Major wire services such as Reuters or AP may be used for confirmation if needed.
  • Resolution Date: This question resolves when CAF officially confirms Morocco’s semifinal opponent, expected by 14 January 2026.
  • Accepted Answer Format: Provide the common English short country name of the team listed as Morocco’s semifinal opponent.

The Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON) is Africa’s continental men’s football championship. DR Congo are one of the teams competing, and knockout matches decide which side advances. criteria:
  • Official Confederation of African Football (CAF) match records or tournament bracket for the knockout match in which DR Congo are eliminated. If needed, major news reports may be used as secondary confirmation.
  • This question resolves on the date DR Congo are officially eliminated from AFCON 2025, expected in January 2026.
  • Provide the short English team name exactly as used in official competition coverage.

This question concerns any penalty awarded to Morocco during stoppage time of normal play, before extra time. criteria:
  • Source of Truth: Resolve using the official CAF match report for the final. If the taker of the missed penalty is not clearly identified there, use contemporaneous Reuters or Associated Press match reports as secondary confirmation.
  • Resolution Date: The question resolves when the match reports describing the penalty incident are publicly available, expected by 19 January 2026.
  • Accepted Answer Format: Provide the full name of the Moroccan player who takes that stoppage-time penalty and fails to score.

  • Resolution Date: This question resolves when that appeal intention is publicly announced, no later than 31 March 2026.
  • Accepted Answer Format: Provide the single country name whose football federation says it will make that appeal.

Donald Trump has said the US will “run” Venezuela until a “safe” transition of power can take place after carrying out strikes on the country and capturing its president. The US president said Nicolás Maduro and his wife Cilia Flores had been flown out of Caracas and indicted on “narco-terrorism” charges following the overnight operation. The operation followed months of pressure from Washington on the oil-rich South American nation, which Mr Trump said the US would run until a “safe, proper and judicious transition” could take place. “We don’t want to be involved with having somebody else get in and we have the same situation that we had for the last long period of years, so we are going to run the country until such time as we can do a safe, proper and judicious transition,” Mr Trump told a news conference. “And it has to be judicious, because that’s what we’re all about. “We want peace, liberty and justice for the great people of Venezuela, and that includes many from Venezuela that are now living in the United States and want to go back to their country, it’s their homeland.” He said large American oil companies will also “go in, spend billions of dollars, fix the badly broken infrastructure, the oil infrastructure, and start making money for the country”. Shortly before taking questions from media, the US president also shared an image on his Truth Social platform which he claimed showed the Venezuelan president “on board the USS Iwo Jima”. In a statement via his spokesman, the UN secretary general voiced concerns that the rules of international law had not been respected. [ Why has US attacked Caracas and captured Venezuela’s president?Opens in new window ] Stephane Dujarric, spokesman for Antonio Guterres, said: “The secretary general is deeply alarmed by the recent escalation in Venezuela, culminating with today’s United States military action in the country, which has potential worrying implications for the region. “Independently of the situation in Venezuela, these developments constitute a dangerous precedent.” Responding to the overnight events in Venezuela before Mr Trump addressed the press, meanwhile, Minister for Foreign Affairs Helen McEntee said: “Ireland, together with our EU partners, underlines the absolute necessity of full respect for international law and the principles of the UN Charter. “While we have been clear that president Maduro does not have any democratic legitimacy, we have consistently called for a peaceful and negotiated transition in Venezuela, and have supported all international efforts to that end. “The Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade

Overnight Friday, the US carried out air strikes across Venezuela, with explosions rocking the capital, Caracas, before dawn. blocks.content:
QID 279531 title: What region will be described as the place where a “big armada” is forming by January 27, 2026? background: Question Start Date: 2026-01-10. In geopolitical reporting, large groupings of naval forces are sometimes described with terms like “armada,” and journalists typically specify the region where such forces are gathering. resolution_criteria:
  • Source of Truth: The question will resolve based on consistent identification of the region in major international news reporting and/or official statements that use the “armada” description and name the region.
  • Resolution Date: January 27, 2026 (end of day, UTC).
  • Accepted Answer Format: The region name as a short phrase (1–3 words), matching the wording used in the confirming report.
options: my_prediction: {"South America": 0.68, "Caribbean": 0.16, "Caribbean Sea": 0.04, "Gulf of Mexico": 0.02, "Eastern Pacific": 0.01} my_prediction_date: 2025-12-31 QID 709715 title: Who will Volodymyr Zelenskyy appoint as Ukraine's economic development adviser by January 6, 2026? background: Question Start Date: 2025-12-14. Ukraine is seeking outside expertise to support wartime economic policy and future investment. resolution_criteria:
  • Source of Truth: An official announcement from the Office of the President of Ukraine or a public statement from the appointee, corroborated by major international news outlets.
  • Resolution Date: This question resolves on the calendar date when the appointment is first publicly announced, no later than 6 January 2026.
  • Accepted Answer Format: Provide the full name of the appointed adviser.
options: my_prediction: {"Kyrylo Budanov": 0.48, "Penny Pritzker": 0.07, "Natalie Jaresko": 0.05, "Aivaras Abromavicius": 0.03, "Oleksandr Kubrakov": 0.03} my_prediction_date: 2026-01-02 QID 797906 title: What military body will President Rashad al-Alimi announce to integrate anti-Houthi forces into Yemen’s military by January 2026? background: Question Start Date: 2025-12-14. Yemen’s internationally recognised government is trying to unify multiple armed factions that oppose the Houthis. A new command structure may be announced as part of that effort. resolution_criteria:
  • Source of Truth: Official statements from Yemen’s internationally recognised presidency or state media, corroborated by major international reporting such as Reuters, AP, or Al Jazeera.
  • Resolution Date: This question resolves on the date, no later than 31 January 2026, when the new military body is publicly announced and named.
  • Accepted Answer Format: Provide the exact official English name of the newly announced body. The answer should be a short organizational name, not an acronym unless that is the only official form used in the announcement.
options: my_prediction: {"National Shield Forces": 0.34, "National Shield forces": 0.22, "Homeland Shield forces": 0.16, "Homeland Shield Forces": 0.1, "Nation's Shield Forces": 0.06} my_prediction_date: 2026-01-02 QID 69214 title: Which Iranian province will state television say lost 30 police and security personnel during the protests, by 11th January 2026? background: Question Start Date: 2026-01-01. Protest activity is taking place in multiple parts of Iran. State media and official outlets may later identify where reported casualties among security forces are concentrated. resolution_criteria:
  • The question resolves based on Iranian state television or state media reports, or on reliable news coverage clearly attributing the claim to Iranian state television.
  • The question resolves when the relevant state television report is publicly available, and no later than 11th January 2026.
  • Provide the province name only, in standard English transliteration.
options: my_prediction: {"Lorestan": 0.37, "Chaharmahal and Bakhtiari": 0.16, "Tehran": 0.11, "Isfahan": 0.07, "Sistan and Baluchestan": 0.05} my_prediction_date: 2026-01-02 QID 663866 title: Which country will publicly protest the release on bail of the Crans-Montana bar owner by February 2026? background: Question Start Date: 2026-01-14. The deadly Crans-Montana bar fire affects victims from more than one country. A European state may react publicly to Swiss legal decisions connected to the investigation. resolution_criteria:
  • Public statements from the relevant national authorities or prosecutors, or consistent reporting from major wire services such as Reuters or AFP describing which country protested the bail release.
  • Resolve by 1st February 2026, using the first clear public identification of the country that protested the bail decision.
  • Provide the country name only, in English.
options: my_prediction: {"Italy": 0.4, "France": 0.22, "United Arab Emirates": 0.05, "Britain": 0.04, "Portugal": 0.04} my_prediction_date: 2026-01-02 QID 45659 title: Which country will become the first in the world to formally ban the AI chatbot Grok by 12 January 2026? background: Question Start Date: 2026-01-01. Governments are debating how to regulate generative AI chatbots that can create realistic text and images. If a country formally bans such a tool, the decision is likely to be announced by an official government body and then reported by major news outlets. resolution_criteria:
  • Source of Truth: Official statements, orders, or announcements from national governments or national regulators, with corroboration from major international news reporting.
  • Resolution Date: This question resolves when a country is publicly identified as the first country to have formally banned Grok, on or before 12 January 2026.
  • Accepted Answer Format: The short English name of the country that is identified as first.
options: my_prediction: {"India": 0.32, "France": 0.22, "Turkey": 0.12, "Australia": 0.06, "Germany": 0.04} my_prediction_date: 2026-01-02 QID 465445 title: Who will Tyson Fury announce as the opponent for his April 11, 2026 comeback fight by January 31, 2026? background: Question Start Date: 2025-12-31. Tyson Fury is a former heavyweight boxing champion. Boxing fans are watching for any official announcement about a possible return bout in 2026. resolution_criteria:
  • Source of Truth: This question resolves using the first official public announcement from Tyson Fury, his verified representatives, his promoter, or the event broadcaster that names the opponent for Fury's scheduled April 11, 2026 comeback fight. Major news wires may be used only to confirm the contents of that official announcement.
  • Resolution Date: The question resolves on the date that the opponent is first officially announced, no later than January 31, 2026.
  • Accepted Answer Format: Provide the opponent's full name exactly as given in the announcement.
options: my_prediction: {"Anthony Joshua": 0.19, "Oleksandr Usyk": 0.17, "Deontay Wilder": 0.13, "Dillian Whyte": 0.08, "Fabio Wardley": 0.07} my_prediction_date: 2026-01-01 QID 655453 title: Which country will mediate the first face-to-face three-way talks among the US, Ukraine and Russia since the 2022 invasion, in January 2026? background: Question Start Date: 2025-12-31. New diplomacy is under way around the war in Ukraine, including discussion of a direct meeting involving US, Ukrainian and Russian delegations. The mediator's country matters because it provides the diplomatic setting and framework for the talks. resolution_criteria:
  • Official communiques or readouts from the mediating government and from the US, Ukrainian or Russian delegations, corroborated by major news agencies such as Reuters or AP, will determine which country is acting as mediator.
  • The question resolves when the first such three-way talks are publicly confirmed in January 2026.
  • Provide the mediator country's full common English name, in 1-3 words.
options: my_prediction: {"Turkey": 0.36, "Saudi Arabia": 0.15, "Qatar": 0.1, "United Arab Emirates": 0.05, "Switzerland": 0.04} my_prediction_date: 2026-01-02 QID 48762 title: Which city will host three-way talks between officials from Ukraine, Russia, and the United States on January 23-24, 2026? background: Question Start Date: 2026-01-15. Diplomats connected to the war between Russia and Ukraine are discussing possible face-to-face talks involving multiple governments in late January 2026. resolution_criteria:
  • Source of Truth: Official schedules or public statements from the governments involved, or, if those are unclear, consistent reporting from major international news organizations identifying the host city for the planned January 23-24, 2026 talks.
  • Resolution Date: This question resolves when the host city for those talks is publicly identified, and no later than 24 January 2026.
  • Accepted Answer Format: City name only, in common English usage.
options: my_prediction: {"Istanbul": 0.3, "Ankara": 0.18, "Riyadh": 0.14, "Doha": 0.06, "Geneva": 0.05} my_prediction_date: 2026-01-02 QID 575662 title: Where will the second round of US-brokered Russia-Ukraine peace talks begin on February 4, 2026? background: Question Start Date: 2026-01-19. The United States is seeking to broker talks between Russia and Ukraine to try to end the war. A second round of trilateral negotiations is expected in early February 2026. resolution_criteria:
  • Source of Truth: official statements from the Ukrainian delegation, Russian delegation, US government representatives, or consistent reporting from at least two major international news organizations.
  • Resolution Date: 4 February 2026
  • Accepted Answer Format: the city name where the talks are reported to begin, written as a short location name.
options: my_prediction: {"Istanbul": 0.34, "Riyadh": 0.13, "Ankara": 0.1, "Doha": 0.07, "Geneva": 0.06} my_prediction_date: 2026-01-02 QID 459037 title: Which country will face host Morocco in the AFCON 2025 semifinal in Rabat on 14 January 2026? background: Question Start Date: 2025-12-19. The Africa Cup of Nations is Africa’s main men’s international football tournament. As the knockout rounds unfold, the bracket will determine which team meets the host nation in the semifinal. resolution_criteria:
  • Source of Truth: CAF’s official AFCON 2025 bracket and match schedule on CAFOnline.com. Major wire services such as Reuters or AP may be used for confirmation if needed.
  • Resolution Date: This question resolves when CAF officially confirms Morocco’s semifinal opponent, expected by 14 January 2026.
  • Accepted Answer Format: Provide the common English short country name of the team listed as Morocco’s semifinal opponent.
options: my_prediction: {"Nigeria": 0.47, "Algeria": 0.42, "DR Congo": 0.06, "Mozambique": 0.02, "Burkina Faso": 0.01} my_prediction_date: 2026-01-01 QID 703511 title: Which team will knock DR Congo out of AFCON 2025 in January 2026? background: Question Start Date: 2025-12-19. The Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON) is Africa’s continental men’s football championship. DR Congo are one of the teams competing, and knockout matches decide which side advances. resolution_criteria:
  • Official Confederation of African Football (CAF) match records or tournament bracket for the knockout match in which DR Congo are eliminated. If needed, major news reports may be used as secondary confirmation.
  • This question resolves on the date DR Congo are officially eliminated from AFCON 2025, expected in January 2026.
  • Provide the short English team name exactly as used in official competition coverage.
options: my_prediction: {"Algeria": 0.85, "Nigeria": 0.03, "Morocco": 0.02, "Burkina Faso": 0.01, "Senegal": 0.01} my_prediction_date: 2026-01-01 QID 75613 title: In which country will Representative Thomas Massie say Venezuelan oil proceeds are being banked overseas by February 28, 2026? background: Question Start Date: 2026-01-12. Members of Congress are debating whether money connected to foreign oil transactions can be held outside the United States and outside the normal congressional spending process. A lawmaker may publicly name a foreign country linked to such an account. resolution_criteria:
  • Source of Truth: Thomas Massie's official public statements, including verified social media posts or congressional communications, with confirmation from major news reports
  • Resolution Date: February 28, 2026
  • Accepted Answer Format: string (location)
options: my_prediction: {"Switzerland": 0.14, "Qatar": 0.1, "China": 0.08, "United Arab Emirates": 0.06, "Russia": 0.04} my_prediction_date: 2025-12-24 QID 599463 title: Which governor will the US Department of Justice open an investigation into by January 31, 2026, for alleged conspiracy to impede immigration enforcement? background: Question Start Date: 2026-01-09. Conflicts between state leaders and the federal government over immigration enforcement can escalate into formal legal or political investigations. This question asks which governor will later be publicly named in such a federal investigation. resolution_criteria:
  • Source of Truth: This question resolves based on an official US Department of Justice announcement, court filings, or consistent reporting from major news organizations such as AP, Reuters, or comparable national outlets.
  • Resolution Date: The question resolves no later than January 31, 2026, once the governor is publicly named in an investigation fitting the description.
  • Accepted Answer Format: Provide the governor’s full name as publicly reported.
options: my_prediction: {"Tim Walz": 0.3, "JB Pritzker": 0.14, "Gavin Newsom": 0.08, "Maura Healey": 0.06, "Kathy Hochul": 0.05} my_prediction_date: 2026-01-02 QID 915183 title: Which US state will officials describe as the site of the largest federal immigration enforcement operation ever carried out by January 25, 2026? background: Question Start Date: 2025-11-30. The US federal government is conducting immigration enforcement operations in multiple jurisdictions. Officials may later characterize one state-level operation as unprecedented in scale. resolution_criteria:
  • statements from federal officials and reporting from major news organizations published by January 25, 2026.
  • this question resolves when the state tied to that description is clearly named in those sources, no later than January 25, 2026.
  • provide the name of a US state only.

input.command:
/usr/bin/bash -lc "python - <<'PY'
import json,re
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('articles/2026/01/03/articles.jsonl')
terms=['big armada','armada','economic development adviser','Budanov','chief of staff','Crans-Montana','Grok','National Shield','Homeland Shield','Anthony Joshua','Tyson Fury','Iran protests','Lorestan','Chaharmahal','bail']
arts=[]
for line in p.open():
    a=json.loads(line)
    if a.get('date_publish','9999-99-99')>'2026-01-03':
        continue
    text=(a.get('title','')+'\\n'+a.get('content',''))
    low=text.lower()
    hits=[t for t in terms if t.lower() in low]
    if hits:
        arts.append((hits,a))
for hits,a in arts[:80]:
    content=' '.join(a.get('content','').split())
    print('\\n---',a.get('date_publish'),a.get('source'),hits)
    print(a.get('title'))
    for h in hits[:2]:
        m=re.search(re.escape(h), content, re.I)
        if m:
            s=max(0,m.start()-280); e=min(len(content),m.end()+420)
            print('...',content[s:e],'...')
            break
PY"

# Forecast notes - 2025-12-31

State check: `state.json` absent. `market.csv` had 330 active/unresolved questions, all with active predictions. Local corpus file `articles/2025/12/31/articles.jsonl` had 2794 articles. One `search_news` call for Morocco calf injury timed out after 120s, so I relied on local article searches.

Submitted updates:

queries=['Morocco semifinal','Rabat on 14 January','Morocco quarterfinal','Morocco coach warns','South Africa Cameroon','Nigeria','Algeria DR Congo','DR Congo Algeria','AFCON bracket','semifinal in Rabat']
for q in queries:
    print('\\nQUERY',q)
    n=0
    for line in p.open(errors='ignore'):
        a=json.loads(line)
        if a.get('date_publish','9999-99-99')>'2026-01-03': continue
        title=a.get('title') or ''; content=' '.join((a.get('content') or '').split())
        text=title+' '+content
        if re.search(re.escape(q), text, re.I):

            print('---',a.get('date_publish'),a.get('source'),title)
            print(text[s:e])
            if n>=5: break
    print('shown',n)
PY"

/usr/bin/bash -lc "python - <<'PY'
import json,re
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('articles/2026/01/03/articles.jsonl')
queries=['Morocco semifinal','Rabat on 14 January','Morocco quarterfinal','Morocco coach warns','South Africa Cameroon','Nigeria','Algeria DR Congo','DR Congo Algeria','AFCON bracket','semifinal in Rabat']
for q in queries:
    print('\\nQUERY',q)
    n=0
    for line in p.open(errors='ignore'):
        a=json.loads(line)
        if a.get('date_publish','9999-99-99')>'2026-01-03': continue
        title=a.get('title') or ''; content=' '.join((a.get('content') or '').split())
        text=title+' '+content
        if re.search(re.escape(q), text, re.I):
            n+=1
            m=re.search(re.escape(q), text, re.I); s=max(0,m.start()-350); e=min(len(text),m.end()+650)
            print('---',a.get('date_publish'),a.get('source'),title)
            print(text[s:e])
            if n>=5: break
    print('shown',n)
PY"

input.command:
/usr/bin/bash -lc "python - <<'PY'
import csv,json,re,collections,math
from pathlib import Path
stop=set('the a an and or to of in on for by with from after before as at into about which what who will be is are was were has have had its his her their this that over under amid among during first second new old us u s jan january february march 2026 2025 will say says said report reports reported identify named name country city state province team player government president minister officials authorities public official active question start date resolution'.split())
# collect Jan3 article titles + first content sentence from major sources, date_publish<=Jan3
docs=[]
p=Path('articles/2026/01/03/articles.jsonl')
for line in p.open():
    a=json.loads(line)
    if a.get('date_publish','9999-99-99')>'2026-01-03': continue
    source=a.get('source') or ''
    if not any(s in source for s in ['aljazeera','reuters','straitstimes','irish','independent','cbsnews','skysports','khaleej','hindustantimes','foxnews','ndtv']):
        continue
    text=(a.get('title') or '')+' '+(' '.join((a.get('content') or '').split())[:500])
    toks=set(t.lower() for t in re.findall(r\"[A-Za-z][A-Za-z'-]{2,}\", text)) - stop
    docs.append((toks,a.get('source'),a.get('title')))
rows=list(csv.DictReader(open('market.csv')))
active=[r for r in rows if r['is_resolved']=='False']
scored=[]
for r in active:
    text=' '.join([r['title'],r.get('background',''),r.get('my_prediction','')])
    toks=set(t.lower() for t in re.findall(r\"[A-Za-z][A-Za-z'-]{2,}\", text)) - stop
    toks={t for t in toks if len(t)>3}
    hits=[]
    score=0
    for dt,source,title in docs:
        inter=toks & dt
        # require at least one fairly distinctive exact token from title/prediction
        if len(inter)>=2:
            # boost rare-ish proper nouns manually via token lengths
            val=sum(2 if len(x)>=7 else 1 for x in inter)
            if val>=4:
                hits.append((val,source,title,sorted(inter)))
                score+=val
    if hits:
        hits=sorted(hits, reverse=True)[:3]
        scored.append((score,r['qid'],r['my_prediction_date'],r['title'],hits))
for score,qid,pdate,title,hits in sorted(scored, reverse=True)[:45]:
    print('\\nSCORE',score,'QID',qid,'pred',pdate)
    print(title)
    for val,source,htitle,inter in hits:
        print(' ',val,source,'|',htitle,'|',','.join(inter[:8]))
PY"

input.command:
/usr/bin/bash -lc "python - <<'PY'
import json,re
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('articles/2026/01/03/articles.jsonl')
queries=['multiple explosions','explosions heard','Caracas explosions','Venezuela shaken by series of explosions','capital Caracas','Fuerte Tiuna','La Carlota']
for q in queries:
    print('\\nQUERY',q)
    n=0
    for line in p.open(errors='ignore'):
        a=json.loads(line)
        if a.get('date_publish','9999-99-99')>'2026-01-03': continue
        title=a.get('title') or ''; content=' '.join((a.get('content') or '').split())
        text=title+' '+content
        if re.search(re.escape(q), text, re.I):
            n+=1
            m=re.search(re.escape(q), text, re.I); s=max(0,m.start()-320); e=min(len(text),m.end()+700)
            print('---',a.get('date_publish'),a.get('source'),title)
            print(text[s:e])
            if n>=6: break
    print('shown',n)
PY"

Venezuela shaken by series of explosions in capital Caracas Venezuela was shaken early on Saturday (January 3) as multiple explosions rocked its capital, Caracas, amid soaring tensions with the United States. At least seven blasts were heard around 2 a.m. local time, accompanied by reports of low-flying aircraft and plumes of smoke over several neighbourhoods. Residents described panicked scenes as parts of the city experienced power outages and rushed into the streets. Caracas’s government accused the US of military aggression and declared a state of emergency, saying the strikes targeted both civilian and military sites. The Pentagon and White House did not immediately respond to requests for comment. Smoke could be seen rising from the hangar of a military base in Caracas. Another military installation in the capital was without power. In a statement, Venezuela’s government confirmed that Caracas had come under attack, as well as three other states: Miranda, La Guaira and Aragua. “The only objective of this attack is to seize control of Venezuela’s strategic resources, in particular its oil and minerals,” it said, calling on the international community to denounce what it called a flagrant violation of international law that put millions of lives at risk. Venezuela’s government said the United States attacked its civilian and military installations in multiple states after at least seven explosions and low-flying aircraft were heard early on Saturday in the capital Caracas. Mr Maduro earlier declared a state of emergency and called on “political and social forces” to reject the attacks, according to the statement.             print(text[s:e])
            if n>=5: break
    print('shown',n)
